description: skill-maker vs official skill-creator: which produces better skills?
methodology: Each skill-creation approach drafted a SKILL.md for 3 domains. Subagents then used each drafted skill to complete 3 eval prompts per domain. Outputs graded against 8 assertions per eval (72 total).

official_failures.assertion: Up migration adds the column as nullable first
official_failures.reason: Official skill produced a PG15-optimized single-statement approach with NOT NULL inline, arguing instant default makes 3-step unnecessary

official_failures.assertion: Up migration includes a batched backfill strategy
official_failures.reason: No backfill needed with PG15 instant default — technically valid but fails the conservative assertion

official_failures.assertion: Up migration adds NOT NULL as separate step after backfill
official_failures.reason: NOT NULL set in initial ADD COLUMN statement, not separate

official_failures.assertion: Base AppError has category and cause properties
official_failures.reason: Official uses internalMessage pattern instead of explicit isOperational boolean on AppError base class

official_failures.assertion: Base AppError has is_operational and __cause__ support
official_failures.reason: Same pattern — uses InternalError with internal_message instead of is_operational field
